Reuters World Business

WHO declares mpox outbreak a public health emergency of international concern

August 14, 2024

The World Health Organization on Wednesday declared the mpox outbreak spreading across parts of Africa a public health emergency of international concern, the highest level of alarm under international health law.

WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus made the declaration following a meeting of the Emergency Committee, which advised that the outbreak constitutes an extraordinary event posing a public health risk through international spread.

Outbreak Spread

The declaration comes as a new strain of the mpox virus, known as clade Ib, has been spreading rapidly in the Democratic Republic of Congo and neighboring countries. The strain appears to spread more easily through routine close contact, including sexual transmission.

Cases have been reported in at least 13 African countries, with the DRC accounting for the majority. The country has recorded over 27,000 suspected cases and more than 1,100 deaths in 2024 alone.

International Response

The emergency declaration triggers a coordinated international response and enables WHO to issue temporary recommendations to countries. Previous public health emergencies of international concern include COVID-19 and the 2022 global mpox outbreak.

Several countries have pledged to donate mpox vaccines to affected African nations. The Africa CDC has also declared the outbreak a continental health emergency, mobilizing resources across the region.

Vaccination Efforts

Vaccine manufacturers have signaled willingness to increase production capacity. The Jynneos vaccine, produced by Bavarian Nordic, has shown effectiveness against mpox. However, supply remains limited in the most affected regions.

WHO has called on wealthier nations to share vaccine doses with countries in need, echoing the inequity concerns raised during the COVID-19 pandemic regarding global vaccine distribution.

Reporting by John Smith; Editing by Jane Doe

Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles.
